project_claim: source-known recompilable bounded-input localized semantic bug auditing

out_of_scope: claiming better decompiler generation quality than SOTA decompilers

negative_evidence.result: naive global binary feature distance failed
negative_evidence.paper_role: motivates why lightweight static/binary distance is insufficient
shows static feature repair did not solve the auditing problem
supports moving away from static motif as the primary method

positive_evidence.paper_role: first strong main-method gate on curated localized bugs
supports that the gain is not label leakage or fixture-only collapse

positive_evidence.result: boundary-preserving dynamic trace on generated candidates
positive_evidence.paper_role: shows signal on LLM-generated candidate distribution
shows method transfers to a new curated source-known function pool
positive smoke on generated candidates, but not sufficient as CCF-A main evidence

known_limits: Current function pools are still small.
Phase 3 GPU generated evidence is smoke/top-up scale, not full-scale evidence.
Real-project source-known transfer has not been run.
Decompiler-output feasibility has not been run.
The current method is an audit signal, not a decompiler generation model.

A SOTA claim requires a baseline matrix against recent decompilation benchmarks, fixture-only/re-execution metrics, static similarity, symbolic or fuzzing-style checks where available, and LLM-judge baselines.

reviewer_risks.answer: Phase 1-3 support feasibility and method discovery only. CCF-A main evidence needs Phase 5/6 full-scale transfer with at least 30 real-project functions, 100 compile-pass candidates, and 20 paired functions.
The paper should not claim SOTA decompiler generation. It must claim a stronger auditing signal for localized semantic drift and prove this against baselines.
Every paper artifact must state that original source remains the oracle.
Primary score must not use fixture_mismatch_rate, and fixture_collapse must remain false.

baseline_families_required: symbolic/concolic or fuzzing-inspired checks when dependencies are available
LLM judge or LLM self-repair baseline if used in candidate generation
real decompiler or decompiler-benchmark metrics when Phase 6 is active
